title: Production Reports
learning_objectives: Understand production reporting in SAP PP.
Learn when each report should be used.
Interpret production planning reports for decision making.

introduction.title: What are Production Reports?
introduction.content: Production Reports provide real-time visibility into manufacturing operations. They help planners, supervisors and management monitor production orders, material availability, production progress, delays, capacity utilization and inventory status. SAP offers standard reports while organizations like JSL also use customized Z-reports for plant-specific reporting.

business_purpose.title: Why are Production Reports Important?
business_purpose.content: Production reports support production planning, monitoring and decision-making. They help identify bottlenecks, track production progress, monitor inventory and ensure timely completion of manufacturing activities.

standard_reports.purpose: Displays production orders based on different selection criteria.
Displays stock availability and material requirements.
Displays planning results generated by MRP.
Displays material stock by plant and storage location.

jsl_reports.purpose: Displays production planning information as per JSL business requirements.
Monitors production execution and order status.
Displays production progress and pending activities.
Tracks execution status of production orders.
Supports production analysis and operational review.
Provides summarized production information for management review.

business_users.purpose: Uses reports for production planning and scheduling.

step_by_step_procedure: Specify Material or Production Order if required.
best_practices: Analyze MRP exceptions before releasing orders.
Use reports for production review meetings.
important_notes: Standard SAP reports are available in every SAP system.
ZPP reports are organization-specific custom developments.
Access to reports depends on user authorization.
Production reports support operational and management decision-making.
